app: showclock — paste-and-go run-of-show timer with live drift + reflow + share URL
note: All 10 personas copied verbatim from the canonical roster; one app-specific 'motivation' added each, phrased from the persona's real recurring workflow.

personas.name: Priya
personas.stack: terminal + neovim, GitHub, Kubernetes dashboards, Datadog, Linear, Slack
personas.temperament: skeptical of new tools; hates signups; will inspect network tab if suspicious; abandons anything slower than a CLI
personas.motivation: Runs the team's monthly internal demo day — 8 lightning talks back-to-back. Needs a no-signup timer that keeps the slot times honest and that she can confirm is fully client-side (will check the network tab) before trusting it on the projector.

personas.name: Marcus
personas.stack: VS Code, GitHub, Vercel, Figma dev mode, Storybook, Discord/Slack
personas.temperament: enthusiastic early adopter; shares tools in team Slack when impressed; notices janky CSS instantly
personas.motivation: MCs the bi-weekly sprint demo and the annual hackathon showcase. Wants to paste the agenda once, drop the presenter link in the team Slack so a co-host has it, and have the big-type countdown look clean on a shared screen.

personas.name: Wen
personas.stack: BigQuery/SQL, Google Sheets, Looker Studio, dbt, GA4, Slack
personas.temperament: lives in data hygiene; distrusts tools that transform data invisibly; wants CSV in/out everywhere
personas.motivation: Leads a weekly 6-segment data-literacy training for the marketing org. Wants the parsed rundown to match exactly what she pasted (no silent reinterpretation of her durations) and the drift math to be transparent.

personas.name: Tomás
personas.role: Operations analyst at a mid-size company
personas.stack: Excel (power user), Tableau, Jira, Teams, SharePoint
personas.temperament: pragmatic; IT blocks installs so browser tools appeal; wary of pasting company data into random sites
personas.motivation: Facilitates the recurring weekly ops review with a strict 45-minute agenda. IT blocks desktop installs, so a browser tool that needs no login and keeps no data on a server is exactly his lane.

personas.name: Dana
personas.device: MacBook in cafes; phone between meetings
personas.stack: HubSpot, GA4, Notion, Canva, LinkedIn Campaign Manager, Google Ads
personas.temperament: ruthless about time; if value isn't obvious in one scroll she's gone; screenshots tools she likes for the team channel
personas.motivation: Hosts monthly product webinars with a tight segment schedule (intro, demo, guest, Q&A). Needs to glance at a phone or second screen and instantly know if she's running behind so she can trim Q&A.

personas.name: Jules
personas.stack: Notion, Buffer, Mailchimp, Figma (consuming), X/LinkedIn/Mastodon, Discord
personas.temperament: posts constantly, juggles platform quirks daily; allergic to anything requiring a login for a small job
personas.motivation: Runs a weekly community AMA / live stream with a loose run of show. Wants a zero-login timer she can open on her phone podium and a read-only link to hand a co-host.

personas.name: Aisha
personas.stack: Figma, FigJam, Notion, Loom, Maze, Slack
personas.temperament: judges craft hard — spacing, copy tone, empty states; a clumsy UI loses her regardless of utility; advocates loudly for tools that feel considered
personas.motivation: Facilitates timeboxed design workshops in FigJam (5-min warmup, 15-min sketch, 10-min critique). Lives or dies on timeboxes and judges whether the presenter view is calm and legible enough to glance at while running the room.

personas.name: Rob
personas.stack: Figma, Illustrator, Photoshop, Google Drive, client Slacks/emails
personas.temperament: does repetitive asset-prep grunt work weekly; price-sensitive; compares everything to 'I could do this in Photoshop in 4 minutes'
personas.motivation: Runs paced client presentation walkthroughs and the occasional portfolio-review panel. Price-sensitive — will weigh this against just glancing at his phone clock, so the drift/reflow has to clearly earn its place.

personas.name: Elena
personas.device: laptop + phone, half the day in meetings
personas.stack: Google Calendar (wall-to-wall), Slack, Linear, Google Docs, Zoom, dashboards she skims
personas.temperament: 30-second patience budget; uses tools between meetings on her phone; anything requiring setup is dead to her; recommends tools that save her reports time
personas.motivation: Runs the weekly team all-hands and a monthly retro, both with rigid agendas she always overruns. Has a 30-second patience budget — if pasting the agenda and starting isn't near-instant she's back to the calendar clock.

personas.name: Sam
personas.stack: Notion, Sheets, Slack, Zoom, Asana, Amplitude (skims), endless browser tabs
personas.temperament: synthesizes and shares constantly — exports, links, screenshots; values anything that makes him look organized; mild tech confidence, won't debug anything
personas.motivation: Facilitates sprint planning and quarterly roadmap workshops. Wants to look organized — paste the agenda, share the link, and have remaining segments visibly reflow when one runs long so the room trusts the schedule.
